topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

My phone buzzed, and my breath hitched. A notification. It was from Mark. Mark, the architect of scraped knees and whispered insults in the third grade. A friend request. My fingers hovered over the screen, a strange, buzzing energy thrumming beneath my skin. I tapped it. The little profile picture was a slightly blurry selfie from a summer camp, and a slow, wide smile spread across my face. "Well, look who finally decided to show up," I typed back, the words feeling light and breezy. I couldn't stop grinning. This was… unexpected. I started composing a message, my mind racing with possibilities, picturing what kind of chaos we could stir up.

[story 2]

The screen lit up with the familiar name, and a jolt went through me. It was him. Liam. The one who used to make my stomach clench just thinking about his shadow. A friend request. My heart started doing a quick little tap dance against my ribs. I leaned back in my chair, a wide, almost giddy expression pulling at my lips. I immediately started scrolling through his profile, my eyes darting over every picture, cataloging everything. A surge of pure excitement washed over me; this was a curveball I hadn't seen coming, and I was ready to jump right in. I typed a short, playful greeting, my thumbs flying across the keyboard with a speed born of sheer anticipation.

[story 3]

When the notification popped up, I almost dropped my mug. It was Mark. The kid who had perfected the art of making me feel small. A friend request. A warm flush crept up my neck, and I couldn't help but laugh—a short, bright sound that surprised even me. I snatched my phone, my movements quick and purposeful. I reread his username twice, a genuine spark igniting in my chest. I began drafting a response, my thoughts tumbling over each other in a cascade of eager ideas. I felt a buoyant lightness, as if I were about to launch into the most interesting adventure of the week.
